name: Eavesdropping on Space Communications
description: An adversary intercepts and records downlink or crosslink radio-frequency transmissions from a spacecraft to gather mission data, telemetry parameters, or command acknowledgement patterns. Unencrypted or weakly-encrypted RF links are particularly vulnerable.
detection_guidance: Deploy RF spectrum monitoring at ground stations to detect unexpected receivers in the ground track footprint. Alert on anomalous SNR degradation that could indicate a nearby intercept platform. Correlate downlink traffic volume with known scheduled contacts.
mitigation_guidance: Encrypt all downlink and uplink communications using AES-256 or ECIES. Implement Perfect Forward Secrecy for session keys. Apply frequency hopping or spread-spectrum techniques where mission constraints allow. Classify link frequencies and restrict publication of TLE ephemeris where possible.

name: Gather Spacecraft Configuration Information
description: An adversary collects publicly-available or leaked information about spacecraft bus configuration, software versions, APID assignments, and onboard computer architecture to plan a targeted attack. Sources include regulatory filings, academic publications, procurement documents, and conference presentations.
detection_guidance: Monitor for unusual access patterns to internal technical documentation repositories. Track which staff access sensitive bus configuration documents and correlate with subsequent anomalous behaviour.
mitigation_guidance: Apply information classification policies to spacecraft bus and software documentation. Redact sensitive APID and frequency assignments from public regulatory filings where permitted. Conduct regular reviews of publicly-released technical content for inadvertent disclosure.

name: Ground Station Network Scanning
description: An adversary scans the network-facing interfaces of ground station infrastructure to enumerate open ports, running services, and software versions. This reconnaissance phase precedes exploitation of identified vulnerabilities in mission control, antenna control units, or baseband processing systems.
detection_guidance: Deploy intrusion detection systems (IDS) and honeypots on ground station DMZ segments. Alert on systematic port-scan patterns, abnormal SYN-flood traffic, or unexpected DNS lookups from external IP ranges. Monitor firewall logs for repeated connection attempts to non-standard ports.
mitigation_guidance: Minimise the external attack surface by removing all non-essential public-facing services. Place mission-critical ground segment systems behind a segmented DMZ with strict firewall rules. Implement geo-blocking for countries from which no legitimate traffic is expected.

name: Monitor Space Situational Awareness Data
description: An adversary aggregates publicly-available Space Situational Awareness (SSA) data, including TLE orbital elements, conjunction warnings, and manoeuvre notifications, to predict satellite ground-track visibility windows and schedule attack windows with higher probability of success.
detection_guidance: This technique relies on open-source data and is difficult to detect directly. Monitor for correlation between adversary activity and satellite visibility windows. Track if anomalous ground station contact attempts coincide with orbital pass times.
mitigation_guidance: Restrict publication of precise manoeuvre plans and non-standard orbital parameters in open SSA catalogues where mission operations permit. Randomise or delay non-critical telemetry downlink schedules to decouple predictable pass windows from operational activities.

name: OSINT Collection on Space Operators
description: An adversary uses open-source intelligence techniques to profile satellite operators, mission control staff, and ground segment contractors. Social media, LinkedIn, conference proceedings, and job postings are harvested to identify key personnel, technology stacks, shift patterns, and supplier relationships for subsequent social engineering.
detection_guidance: Monitor dark-web forums and threat intelligence feeds for mentions of the organisation or key personnel. Track unusual spikes in profile views for sensitive technical staff on professional networks.
mitigation_guidance: Provide security awareness training covering OSINT risks. Implement policies governing what technical information staff may share on social media and at conferences. Conduct periodic review of the organisation's external digital footprint.

name: Side-Channel Monitoring of Ground Infrastructure
description: An adversary uses electromagnetic emissions, acoustic signals, power-consumption analysis, or timing side-channels from ground station equipment to infer cryptographic key material, command sequences, or system state without direct network access.
detection_guidance: Physical intrusion detection around critical ground station racks. Monitor for RF emissions from equipment rooms using spectrum analysers. Correlate physical access logs with cryptographic key-rotation events.
mitigation_guidance: Apply TEMPEST-grade RF shielding to cryptographic processing equipment. Use constant-time cryptographic implementations. Enforce strict physical security perimeters around antenna control units and key management hardware.

name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
description: An adversary inserts malicious code or a backdoor into flight software, ground segment software, or a third-party library during the development, build, or distribution phase. The compromise may target mission-critical onboard software updates or ground segment components shared across multiple operators.
detection_guidance: Implement software bill-of-materials (SBOM) tracking and verify cryptographic hashes of all flight software artefacts before upload. Scan ground segment dependencies with vulnerability databases. Monitor build pipelines for unexpected artefact modifications.
mitigation_guidance: Enforce code-signing for all flight software patches. Use reproducible builds and verify hashes out-of-band. Maintain a trusted software baseline and compare against known-good images before any upload. Apply vendor vetting and contractual security requirements for all software suppliers.

name: Deploy Rogue Ground Station
description: An adversary deploys an unauthorised radio frequency ground station within the satellite's ground-track footprint to transmit forged telecommands or malicious uplink frames. The rogue station may masquerade as a legitimate tracking station or exploit open frequency assignments.
detection_guidance: Monitor for unexpected command acknowledgements or telemetry anomalies during pass windows when no legitimate uplink is scheduled. Deploy RF direction-finding to detect unlicensed transmitters in the ground track footprint. Implement cryptographic command authentication to reject unauthenticated uplinks.
mitigation_guidance: Require cryptographic authentication (e.g., CMAC or DSA) for all telecommands. Implement replay-prevention sequence counters. Monitor regulatory RF databases for unlicensed ground station applications in your operational regions.

name: Spear Phishing Ground Operations Staff
description: An adversary sends targeted phishing emails to satellite operations personnel to steal credentials for mission control systems, VPN gateways, or satellite management portals. Emails may impersonate spacecraft manufacturers, regulatory agencies, or partner operators.
detection_guidance: Deploy email gateway with sandboxing and URL rewriting for all inbound messages. Alert on credential harvesting domains that mimic internal naming conventions. Monitor authentication logs for logins from unexpected geographies or devices following phishing campaigns.
mitigation_guidance: Enforce phishing-resistant multi-factor authentication (FIDO2/WebAuthn) on all mission-critical systems. Conduct regular phishing simulation exercises. Implement DMARC, DKIM, and SPF for all organisation domains.

name: Exploit Public-Facing Ground Station Services
description: An adversary exploits a known or zero-day vulnerability in a public-facing ground station service such as a web-based mission control interface, SNMP daemon, FTP server, or remote-access portal to obtain initial foothold in the ground segment network.
detection_guidance: Deploy a web application firewall (WAF) in front of public-facing portals. Alert on exploit patterns (SQL injection, path traversal, RCE payloads) in web logs. Monitor for privilege escalation events shortly after external authentication.
mitigation_guidance: Remove all non-essential public-facing services. Apply timely vulnerability patches. Enforce network segmentation so that a compromised DMZ host cannot directly reach mission-critical ground segment systems.

name: Compromise Third-Party Launch Vehicle Software
description: An adversary compromises the software stack of a launch vehicle provider or a shared launch manifest system to introduce malicious payloads into the spacecraft prior to launch or to corrupt separation sequences, causing mission failure or unauthorised payload activation.
detection_guidance: Require independent software integrity verification of all launch vehicle interfaces and spacecraft pre-launch software loads. Audit the launch vehicle provider's software security practices as part of mission assurance reviews.
mitigation_guidance: Conduct independent code review and cryptographic hash verification of all interface control software with launch vehicles. Negotiate contractual security obligations with launch service providers covering software supply chain integrity.

name: Insider Threat via Privileged Ground Staff
description: A malicious or coerced insider with privileged access to mission control systems abuses their position to exfiltrate mission data, insert malicious flight software, or sabotage spacecraft operations. The insider may act alone or in coordination with an external threat actor.
detection_guidance: Implement user and entity behaviour analytics (UEBA) to detect deviations from baseline behaviour patterns. Alert on out-of-hours access to mission-critical systems, large data downloads, or access to assets beyond the user's normal role.
mitigation_guidance: Apply least-privilege access controls and mandatory dual-person authorisation for high-risk operations. Conduct background screening for staff with privileged access. Implement activity logging and regular access reviews.

name: Telecommand Replay Attack
description: An adversary records a legitimate telecommand uplink and retransmits it at a later time to execute an unauthorised spacecraft manoeuvre, mode change, or payload activation. Replay attacks are effective against systems lacking sequence counters or cryptographic freshness tokens.
detection_guidance: Monitor for duplicate telecommand sequence numbers in the spacecraft command verification telemetry. Alert on command acknowledgements received outside of scheduled uplink windows. Detect unexpected mode transitions in housekeeping telemetry.
mitigation_guidance: Implement monotonically-increasing sequence counters in all telecommand frames. Apply cryptographic message authentication codes (CMAC/HMAC) with embedded timestamps. Reject any command with a sequence number below the last accepted value.

name: Upload Malicious Flight Software Patch
description: An adversary exploits the flight software update mechanism to upload a crafted patch that introduces a backdoor, modifies critical safety limits, disables telemetry encryption, or triggers a denial-of-service condition in the onboard computer. The patch may bypass verification checks through code-signing key compromise or exploit an update logic flaw.
detection_guidance: Compare cryptographic hashes of uploaded patches against the approved baseline before activation. Monitor for unexpected increases in memory usage or CPU load following a software update. Alert on housekeeping telemetry deviations in the first orbit after a patch upload.
mitigation_guidance: Enforce code-signing for all flight software images and patches with keys held in Hardware Security Modules. Require dual-authorisation for flight software uploads. Implement onboard secure-boot verification of all uploaded code before execution.

name: RF Jamming
description: An adversary transmits broadband or narrowband RF energy on the uplink or downlink frequencies of a spacecraft to prevent legitimate command uplinks from being received or to corrupt downlinked mission data. Jamming may target the command receiver, the TT&C link, or the payload data downlink.
detection_guidance: Monitor for sustained link budget degradation on the TT&C receiver beyond atmospheric and hardware-margin tolerance. Alert on repeated command uplink failures that are not correlated with adverse weather or known RF interference sources. Track SNR trends at ground station antennas.
mitigation_guidance: Implement spread-spectrum or frequency-hopping uplink/downlink designs. Use directional antennas with narrow beamwidths. Define and exercise onboard autonomous safe-mode sequences for periods of communication loss. Work with spectrum regulatory authorities to identify and mitigate sources of interference.

name: GPS/GNSS Spoofing
description: An adversary transmits counterfeit GNSS signals to manipulate the spacecraft's or ground station's perception of position, velocity, and time. Successful spoofing can corrupt attitude determination, invalidate cryptographic timestamps, disrupt orbit maintenance manoeuvres, or cause the spacecraft to execute incorrect ground track coverage.
detection_guidance: Monitor GNSS receiver signal-to-noise ratios for anomalous consistency across all visible satellites (a spoofed signal tends to have uniform SNR). Cross-validate position solutions against independent orbit propagation models. Alert on sudden unexplained position jumps or attitude anomalies correlated with GNSS time.
mitigation_guidance: Deploy multi-constellation GNSS receivers. Implement GNSS anti-spoofing algorithms and cross-validate against onboard star tracker or magnetometer data. Apply authenticated GNSS signals where available (e.g., Galileo OSNMA, GPS military signal).

name: Telecommand Injection via Compromised Ground System
description: An adversary who has obtained access to a ground control system injects unauthorised telecommands directly into the command uplink path. Commands may be injected through the mission control system operator interface, via a compromised command generation tool, or through a privileged backend API, bypassing normal two-person authorisation procedures.
detection_guidance: Capture and audit all telecommand authorisation events in an immutable log. Alert on commands issued outside scheduled contact windows or by accounts that did not perform the pre-command checklist. Monitor for sequences of commands that match no approved procedure.
mitigation_guidance: Enforce dual-person authorisation for all critical telecommand operations. Apply application-layer command verification with cryptographic MACs. Segment the command uplink path from general operations IT so that a compromised workstation cannot reach command uplinking infrastructure.

name: Exploit Onboard Software Vulnerability
description: An adversary exploits a buffer overflow, integer underflow, command parser flaw, or other software vulnerability in the onboard computer's flight software to achieve arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, or denial-of-service. Exploits may be triggered through malformed telecommand frames or specially-crafted inter-system messages.
detection_guidance: Monitor for unexpected software restarts, watchdog resets, or memory fault anomalies in housekeeping telemetry. Alert on anomalous execution flows detected by runtime integrity monitors. Correlate crashes with recently-received non-standard telecommand sequences.
mitigation_guidance: Conduct formal software verification and vulnerability assessment of all flight software prior to launch. Implement memory protection units (MPU) to enforce memory isolation. Apply defensive coding practices (bounds checking, input validation) and enable compiler hardening flags.

name: Modify Onboard Authentication Parameters
description: An adversary modifies the cryptographic keys, sequence counters, or authentication parameters used for command verification in the spacecraft's onboard memory. This enables subsequent execution of arbitrary telecommands without triggering authentication failures, providing a persistent mechanism for spacecraft control.
detection_guidance: Monitor housekeeping telemetry for unexpected changes to authentication parameter table checksums. Implement an onboard audit register that logs all write-operations to protected memory regions. Alert on command sequence counter resets not associated with an authorised re-initialisation.
mitigation_guidance: Store cryptographic authentication keys and sequence counters in hardware-protected memory (e.g., write-once registers, secure elements). Require formal key-management procedures for any key update operation. Implement onboard integrity checks for the authentication parameter table on every boot.

name: Install Software Backdoor in Flight Software
description: An adversary implants a covert code path in the spacecraft's flight software that can be activated by a specific trigger (e.g., a specially-formatted telecommand, a time-based event, or an orbital parameter condition) to execute arbitrary code while evading normal monitoring and command verification.
detection_guidance: Perform binary diffing of in-orbit flight software against the pre-launch baseline image. Monitor for unusual memory access patterns, unexpected network or RF behaviours at trigger times, or anomalous command execution sequences not matching a known procedure.
mitigation_guidance: Maintain cryptographically-signed golden images of all flight software versions. Conduct regular integrity verification of flight software in non-volatile memory against known-good hashes via ground-commanded memory dump. Apply code review policies that require security review for all changes to command dispatch paths.

name: Manipulate Firmware Update Mechanism
description: An adversary exploits or subverts the spacecraft's onboard firmware update capability to permanently embed malicious firmware in non-volatile memory. Unlike a software patch, a firmware-level implant persists across software resets and may survive partial re-imaging attempts.
detection_guidance: Verify firmware checksums against a pre-launch baseline on a regular schedule via commanded memory reads. Monitor for firmware update operations initiated outside of approved maintenance windows. Alert on unexpected increases in boot time which may indicate firmware-level hooking.
mitigation_guidance: Implement hardware-enforced secure boot that verifies firmware cryptographic signatures before execution. Restrict firmware update commands to authenticated sessions with dual authorisation. Retain an independent hardware mechanism to re-flash to a clean factory image.

name: Establish Persistent Ground Station Access
description: After initial access to the ground segment network, an adversary installs persistent mechanisms such as web shells, scheduled tasks, SSH authorized_keys modifications, or VPN backdoors on mission control servers to maintain access across system reboots, credential rotations, and incident response activities.
detection_guidance: Deploy File Integrity Monitoring (FIM) on ground station servers to alert on unauthorised changes to cron jobs, startup scripts, web server directories, and SSH configuration files. Monitor for unusual process spawning from web server processes, which may indicate web shell activity.
mitigation_guidance: Apply configuration management and golden image baselines for all ground station servers. Enforce multi-factor authentication for all remote access. Regularly rotate administrative credentials and SSH host keys. Implement privileged access workstations (PAW) for ground station administration.

name: Modify Stored Mission Data
description: An adversary with access to ground segment storage systems alters archived mission data, telemetry recordings, or software golden images to corrupt the baseline against which current health data is compared, or to conceal evidence of prior intrusion activity.
detection_guidance: Implement write-once or append-only storage for mission telemetry archives. Apply cryptographic hashing (BLAKE2b or SHA-256) to archived data sets and verify hashes on a scheduled basis. Alert on unexpected modification timestamps or hash mismatches.
mitigation_guidance: Store mission telemetry archives in WORM (Write Once Read Many) storage. Implement an independent backup system with offsite replication. Maintain a separate audit trail of all data access and modification events for post-incident forensic analysis.

name: Compromise Key Management Infrastructure
description: An adversary targets the key management system used to generate, store, and distribute cryptographic keys for spacecraft command authentication or data encryption. Compromising key management provides a long-term persistence mechanism that survives flight software updates and command sequence counter resets.
detection_guidance: Monitor HSM audit logs for unexpected key-access or key-export events. Alert on failed administrative authentication attempts to key management systems. Implement anomaly detection on key usage patterns, such as a key being used to authenticate commands at an unusual rate.
mitigation_guidance: Store all spacecraft cryptographic keys in FIPS 140-2 Level 3 or higher HSMs. Enforce dual-control and split-knowledge procedures for key generation and loading. Apply strict network segmentation isolating key management infrastructure from general-purpose IT systems.

name: Denial of Service via RF Flooding
description: An adversary continuously transmits high-power RF energy on the spacecraft uplink frequency to saturate or damage the command receiver, preventing legitimate telecommands from being received. Sustained jamming can force the spacecraft into an autonomous safe-mode while denying operators the ability to recover the mission.
detection_guidance: Monitor receiver automatic gain control (AGC) telemetry for sustained saturation outside of normal atmospheric noise floor. Alert on extended periods of command uplink failure combined with elevated noise temperature on the command receiver. Track command non-acknowledgement rates.
mitigation_guidance: Design spacecraft for graceful degradation to an autonomous safe-mode state that does not require continuous uplink to maintain safe operations. Implement frequency agility or spread-spectrum uplink designs that are resistant to narrowband jamming. Engage telecommunications regulatory authorities to localise and terminate unlicensed RF sources.

name: Payload or Sensor Degradation
description: An adversary issues telecommands that configure a spacecraft's payload or sensors outside of their design operating envelope, causing permanent damage through thermal cycling, detector saturation, optical contamination, or mechanical over-drive. Degradation may be gradual and initially indistinguishable from nominal ageing effects.
detection_guidance: Monitor payload health telemetry for temperatures, current draws, and calibration parameters trending outside their normal operational bounds. Alert on commands that set instrument operating modes beyond approved parameter ranges. Compare calibration stability over time to detect gradual degradation.
mitigation_guidance: Implement onboard parameter range checks that reject commands outside approved flight limit tables. Enforce soft and hard limits on all actuatable parameters with both software and hardware interlocks. Require two-person review for any command that modifies instrument operating limits.

name: Physical Damage via Malicious Command Sequence
description: An adversary issues a deliberate sequence of telecommands designed to cause irreversible physical damage to the spacecraft, such as draining batteries below safe limits, firing thrusters against attitude control to cause structural stress, or discharging pyrotechnic devices out of sequence.
detection_guidance: Implement flight rules engine that blocks commands sequences that would violate operational safety envelopes. Monitor for rapid sequences of safety-critical commands and alert on any attempt to disable hardware interlocks or safety limit checking.
mitigation_guidance: Implement independent hardware interlocks for safety-critical functions such as pyrotechnic devices and propulsion systems that cannot be overridden by software command alone. Require multi-step confirmation sequences for destructive commands. Maintain a flight rules database that is cross-checked against every command sequence before transmission.

name: Exfiltrate Mission Data
description: An adversary with access to the ground segment exfiltrates sensitive mission data, including classified imagery, signals intelligence, cryptographic key material, or proprietary orbital parameters. Data may be exfiltrated via the internet from compromised workstations, via covert RF channels, or through physical media removal.
detection_guidance: Monitor network egress for large data transfers to external destinations not on the approved list. Apply data loss prevention (DLP) policies to mission-critical data repositories. Alert on bulk access to mission data archives outside of normal working hours or by accounts not assigned to data processing roles.
mitigation_guidance: Classify mission data and apply data-at-rest encryption with key management controlled separately from the data store. Implement strict egress filtering on the ground segment network. Apply data labelling and digital rights management for the most sensitive mission products.

name: Disrupt Ground Segment Operations
description: An adversary attacks the availability of ground segment IT and operational technology systems through ransomware deployment, database corruption, or targeted denial-of-service attacks. The goal is to prevent operators from monitoring spacecraft health, uploading commands, or processing mission data.
detection_guidance: Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) tooling on all ground station workstations and servers. Monitor for ransomware-related file system activities such as mass file encryption events. Alert on sudden loss of connectivity to mission control systems from operations consoles.
mitigation_guidance: Maintain offline backups of all mission-critical ground segment configurations and databases. Implement a tested business continuity plan that enables minimum viable operations from a backup site. Apply network segmentation to limit the blast radius of a compromise to isolated segments.

name: Unauthorised Orbit Manoeuvre
description: An adversary issues commands to fire spacecraft propulsion systems to change the spacecraft's orbit without authorisation. This may be used to move the spacecraft into a collision trajectory with another satellite, remove it from its assigned orbital slot, or deplete propellant to shorten the spacecraft's operational life.
detection_guidance: Monitor spacecraft orbital element sets in the SSA catalogue for unexpected changes in orbital parameters. Alert on propulsion-related telemetry anomalies such as valve actuations, tank pressure changes, or attitude disturbances not correlated with a planned manoeuvre. Cross-reference all manoeuvre commands against the approved manoeuvre plan.
mitigation_guidance: Require multi-person authorisation and independent verification via Flight Dynamics before any manoeuvre command is transmitted. Implement onboard checks that compare commanded manoeuvre delta-V against the approved manoeuvre plan. Monitor spacecraft state vectors from SSA networks as an independent check.
